title: Paella

overview.description: A colourful Spanish rice dish with chicken, white fish, prawns and vegetables, gently cooked with saffron and smoked paprika. A wonderful centrepiece meal for sharing.

ingredients: 300g frozen white fish, cut into chunks
method: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan or wide frying pan over a medium-high heat.
Brown the chicken until lightly golden, then remove and set aside.
Cook the onions and peppers for around 8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika and saffron and stir for 30 seconds.
Add the paella rice and stir until well coated.
Return the chicken to the pan.
Pour in the hot chicken stock and season lightly.
Simmer gently without stirring for around 15 minutes.
Arrange the white fish and prawns over the rice and scatter over the peas.
Continue cooking for another 8–10 minutes until the seafood is cooked and the rice is tender.
Increase the heat for the final minute to develop a light socarrat (the crisp rice layer on the bottom), taking care not to burn it.
Remove from the heat, cover loosely with foil and rest for 10 minutes.
Finish with chopped parsley and serve with lemon wedges.

learning.did-you-know: Traditional Valencian paella didn't contain seafood. The mixed seafood versions popular today developed later along Spain's Mediterranean coast.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to cook paella rice by absorption, use saffron sparingly and create a traditional socarrat without burning the rice.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A celebration meal that makes excellent use of frozen seafood while introducing classic Spanish cooking techniques.
chapman-tips: Don't stir the rice once the stock has been added.
Add the seafood towards the end so it stays tender.
Frozen fish and prawns work beautifully in this recipe.
The socarrat—the crisp layer of rice on the bottom—is one of the best parts of a good paella.
notes.batch-cooking: Best cooked fresh. Leftovers can be refrigerated for up to two days and reheated thoroughly, but freezing isn't recommended as the texture of the rice and seafood deteriorates.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently in a frying pan or microwave until piping hot.
